# Layout fixtures

JSON layouts used by `connection-engine` unit tests. Each file describes a
placement set and expected validation / route-graph outcomes.

## Schema

```json
{
  "schemaVersion": 1,
  "catalogueVersion": 1,
  "description": "human-readable purpose",
  "placements": [
    {
      "instanceId": "unique-id",
      "pieceId": "straight-16",
      "x": 0,
      "y": 0,
      "rotation": 0
    }
  ],
  "expect": {
    "valid": true,
    "issues": [{ "severity": "info", "code": "OPEN_END" }],
    "routeGraph": { "closedComponentCount": 1, "openEndCount": 0 }
  }
}
```

- `schemaVersion` — layout JSON format version (`1`).
- `catalogueVersion` — must match `CATALOGUE_V1.version` from piece-catalogue.
- `rotation` — heading step `0–15` (22.5° increments, ADR 003).
- `expect.issues` — match by `severity` and `code` only (order-independent).
- `expect.routeGraph` — optional route-graph assertions.

## Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `two-straights-valid.json` | Colinear straight join |
| `straight-curve-chain-valid.json` | Straight + curve chain |
| `overlap-invalid.json` | Footprint overlap |
| `port-near-miss-invalid.json` | Ports ~0.5 stud apart |
| `port-misaligned-invalid.json` | Coincident ports, wrong facings |
| `curve-loop-valid.json` | Sixteen curves forming a closed loop |
| `quarter-turn-valid.json` | Straight, four curves, straight (90°) |
| `switch-siding-valid.json` | Switch with siding on branch port |

Fixtures are generated by `tests/unit/connection-engine/generate-fixtures.test.ts`
using port-driven placement helpers in `layout-builder.ts`.
